mnemonics: S.L.I.C.E. & D.I.C.E. - Remember list operations by thinking of how you prepare data: Slice, Length, Insert, Concatenate, Extend & Delete, Index, Copy, Enumerate
keyPoints: Lists are mutable sequences that can be modified in-place
Slicing creates a new list copy using list[start:stop:step] syntax
Methods like .append(), .insert(), .extend(), .remove(), and .pop() modify the original list
commonMistakes: Forgetting that list assignment creates a reference (not a copy) - use list.copy() or list[:]
Using the wrong index (off-by-one errors) especially with negative indexing and slicing
Modifying a list while iterating over it, which can cause unexpected behavior
